events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: VFDs & motor controls, field service visit, observed condition is "wrong overload class setting." Work is in-progress. Need to evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue.

Wrong overload class setting on a VFD/motor overload protection is a non-compliant/defective condition. NEC 430 covers motor circuits, overload protection. Wrong overload class could cause nuisance tripping or failure to protect motor. This is a safety issue. Work should not continue until corrected - need to verify correct overload class per motor FLA and application, adjust VFD overload parameters per mfr manual and NEC 430.

Decision: fail because non-compliant and potentially dangerous.
Risk: high (motor protection compromised).
Work stage: in-progress.
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
Percent complete: maybe 50.
Value: 0 (not asked).
Confidence: 0.9.
Findings: wrong overload class setting on VFD/motor overload; non-compliant with NEC 430.
Actions: stop, verify motor FLA and service factor, set correct overload class/parameters per mfr manual, test.
Rationale: overload protection must match motor; wrong class compromises protection.
References: NEC 430, mfr manuals.
